etymology.description: 令(令) (lìng, "order") + 頁(頁) (yè, "head") = order or leadership pertaining to the head -> lead; usher; take
etymology.interpretation: In the traditional form 領(lǐng, "lead"), 令(令) (lìng, "order") serves as the phonetic component, suggesting sound, while 頁(頁) (yè, "head") provides semantic value, indicating a concept related to leadership or guidance.
etymology.simplification: 領(領)→领(領): 頁(頁) (yè, "head") was simplified to 页(頁) for easier writing.

examples.chinese: 她总是选择带头(她總是選擇帶頭)，因为她喜欢领大家去新的高度(因為她喜歡領大家去新的高度)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì xuǎnzé dàitóu, yīnwèi tā xǐhuān lǐng dàjiā qù xīn de gāodù.
examples.english: She always chooses to lead because she likes to usher everyone to new heights.

examples.chinese: 导游会在十点钟领游客去博物馆(導遊會在十點鐘領遊客去博物館)。
examples.pinyin: Dǎoyóu huì zài shí diǎn zhōng lǐng yóukè qù bówùguǎn.
examples.english: The tour guide will take the tourists to the museum at ten o'clock.

examples.chinese: 他在比赛中领着球队获得了胜利(他在比賽中領著球隊獲得了勝利)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài bǐsài zhōng lǐngzhe qiúduì huòdéle shènglì.
examples.english: He led the team to victory in the match.
